parsed.artifact_title: Determination Note: Scheduler Ownership of Event Identity During Continuation

claims.text: Concrete event identity is scheduler-owned and is not accepted from model-authored continuation text.
Run names are human-readable labels and are not treated as durable scheduler identifiers.
Record uniquifiers are bound by the framework after the producing wake completes.

parsed.conclusion: The scheduler owns concrete event identity during continuation. The model is not permitted to author or assert event identity from continuation text. Run names serve as human-readable labels and carry no durable identifier semantics. Record uniquifiers are assigned by the framework only after the producing wake completes. All identity-bearing constructs are scheduler-owned and framework-bound; the model's role is constrained to operating within the identity context the scheduler provides.
declared_losses: The model cannot author or assert concrete event identity during continuation; identity is scheduler-owned and framework-bound. Run names are human-readable only and carry no durable identifier semantics.
parsed.recommended_action: All continuations must defer identity authorship to the scheduler. The model should treat event identity, run names, and record uniquifiers as externally owned and framework-bound. No continuation text should attempt to assert, derive, or persist concrete event identity. Artifacts that depend on identity should reference only scheduler-provided identity fields and should not treat any model-authored label as a durable identifier.
